content: We can reframe second-language vocabulary learning (rooted in linguistic reflection on word meanings and usage) as an iterative self-refinement loop for large language models fine-tuned on educational corpora, where each act of human learner reflection on a word’s context maps to a self-feedback step that polishes the LLM’s ability to generate contextually appropriate vocabulary usage. This framework transforms passive vocabulary instruction into an active, autonomous improvement cycle that leverages both learner and model self-reflection.
